Unstandardize coefficients

unstand_beta.Rd

Given coefficients fitted on a standardized design matrix, recover the coefficients on the original scale by reversing the centering and scaling.

Usage

unstand_beta(b0, b, scale, center, yy_center, L, p)

Arguments

b0

A 1 x L matrix of intercepts (one per lambda).

b

A p x L matrix of coefficients (one column per lambda).

scale

A numeric vector of length p of column standard deviations.

center

A numeric vector of length p of column means.

yy_center

A numeric scalar (the response center, used for Gaussian models).

L

An integer specifying the number of lambda values.

p

An integer specifying the number of predictors.

Value

A (p+1) x L matrix where the first row is the unstandardized intercept and the remaining rows are the unstandardized coefficients.

On this page
